subcodes.title: Words to the Mayor of Berkeley, spoken on 1912-10-07
subcodes.first_line: We must strive until mankind achieves everlasting felicity. Laws are needed which can both preserve the ranks... As these laws are in conformity with the demands of the time, they will unfailingly prevail
subcodes.first_line_orig: باید کوشید تا نوع انسان سعادت ابدی یابد قواعدی در اینخصوص لازم که هم مراتب باقی ماند
subcodes.subjects: @maintainance of degrees in society, @forces of light and darkness; integration and disintegration, @justice ['adl]; social justice and divine justice, @wealth inequality and its moderation, @serving humanity; working for progress, betterment of the world
